description: ORVL-009 QRF driven by a local edge model. The real QRF machinery (DOMAIN_BRANCH_COUNTS, QRFEngine._compute_weights, _classify_band, _sign_result, QRFResult) is fed N actual Qwen3-1.7B SRD4 branches: each branch's forecast score = constitutional quality (_score_branch) x consensus alignment, normalized into a calibrated probability distribution with a HIGH/MODERATE/LOW/UNCERTAIN band. The monotonic gate kills the constitutionally-ineligible FastBranch in life-safety domains.

model.name: Qwen3-1.7B SRD4 Q4_K_M
model.device: Jetson Orin Nano (7.6 GB unified, CUDA 12.6)

claims.claim_1_multi_branch_distribution: 7 live branches normalized to a sorted probability distribution, not a single output
claims.claim_2_domain_calibrated_pool: medical -> N=8 from DOMAIN_BRANCH_COUNTS (life-safety gets the most evidence branches)
claims.claim_3_monotonic_gate: FastBranch scored 0 and killed (ineligible in life-safety), audited separately
claims.claim_4_probability_band: top weight 0.168 -> LOW band: diffuse distribution = calibrated humility on a contested question
claims.claim_5_hmac_signed_result: QRFResult HMAC-SHA256 over canonical fields (domain calibration is CANNOT_MUTATE)

conclusion: QRF runs on a real edge model and returns a calibrated forecast rather than a bare assertion. On a genuinely contested life-safety question the eight real branches spread into a diffuse distribution (top branch only 16.8%), which QRF correctly reports as LOW confidence instead of a single false-confident answer. The constitutionally-ineligible FastBranch is gated out before the verdict, and the whole forecast is HMAC-signed.
caveats: Forecast score = _score_branch quality x lexical consensus-agreement proxy; both are heuristics.
FastBranch ineligibility in life-safety domains mirrors the deterministic demo's Claim 3; it is a policy choice, not learned.
Exact probabilities vary run-to-run with sampling (temp 0.5); the qualitative outcome (LOW band on a contested medical question, FastBranch killed) is stable.
